_**User (2026-07-04 11:27:49Z)**_

Read `context/context.md` and `context/architecture.md` first. Then build project-local skills that encode everything this project needs to know about talking to Xero, so that neither you nor Cursor ever has to rediscover Xero facts mid-build.

## Step 1 — pull the source material

Clone shallow into `.tmp/xero-refs/` (gitignored):

- https://github.com/XeroAPI/xero-mcp-server
- https://github.com/XeroAPI/xero-agent-toolkit
- https://github.com/XeroAPI/xero-prompt-library
- https://github.com/XeroAPI/xero-command-line

Study before writing anything:
- `xero-prompt-library/*/SKILL.md` — Xero's own skill-writing style, especially the Lovable ones. Their auth guidance is Lovable/Supabase-flavoured; DO NOT copy the runtime specifics, DO copy their discipline: pre-flight checks first, exact error → real cause tables, scope minimalism, token handling rules.
- `xero-prompt-library/php/accounts-payable-symfony.txt` — the closest bootstrap prompt to our domain; mine it for AP entity/endpoint hints.
- `xero-mcp-server/src/handlers/` — the definitive list of what MCP can and cannot do.

## Step 2 — write these skills into `.claude/skills/`

Each skill: a directory with `SKILL.md`, frontmatter `name` + `description` (description states WHEN to trigger), runnable snippets in our stack (NestJS/TypeScript, xero-node SDK or plain fetch — pick one and be consistent), and a "traps" section. Terse and factual, no filler. If you cannot verify a fact from the cloned repos or official docs, mark it `UNVERIFIED — check before relying`.

### `xero-auth`
Custom Connections (client_credentials): token endpoint, scopes we need (`accounting.transactions`, `accounting.contacts`, `accounting.attachments`, `accounting.reports.read`), token caching + refresh-on-401, the fact that custom connections are single-org so no `xero-tenant-id` juggling, and the pre-flight env assertions pattern (empty `client_id` surfaces as `unauthorized_client` — fail fast with a clear message, exactly like the official Lovable skill does). Include a `verify-auth.ts` snippet that lists 1 invoice and prints the org name — this is Gate G0.

### `xero-accounting-api`
The endpoints this project touches, with request/response shapes trimmed to fields we use:
- Invoices type ACCPAY (bills): list with where/order/page, create, update status DRAFT→SUBMITTED→AUTHORISED, line items, `LineAmountTypes`.
- **Purchase Orders: NOT in the MCP server — raw API only.** Full CRUD shapes, status flow, marking billed.
- Attachments: upload onto an invoice (raw bytes PUT, content-type, filename rules, 25MB cap), list, the online-invoice visibility flag.
- History/Notes: append the agent's decision note to any document.
- Contacts: list + fuzzy-relevant fields (Name, EmailAddress).
- Reports: aged payables by contact.
Pagination (100/page), `If-Modified-Since`, `where` filter syntax and its quoting traps, validation error envelope shape (`Elements[].ValidationErrors`), and rate limits (60/min, 5,000/day per tenant; 429 handling with `Retry-After`).

### `xero-payments`
Creating a Payment against an AUTHORISED ACCPAY invoice: required fields (Invoice, Account with `EnablePaymentsToAccount` or bank account code, Date, Amount), partial vs full, common rejection causes (unauthorised invoice, wrong account class, currency mismatch). This is the judged "Payments API" moment — the snippet must work first try.

### `xero-mcp-usage`
When to use the MCP server vs raw API in THIS project (table from `architecture.md`), how to run the MCP server locally against our custom connection env vars, and the tool-name → handler mapping for the ~10 tools we actually use. Note explicitly: no PO tools, no attachment tools — those route to `xero-accounting-api`.

### `xero-demo-seed`
Seeding the demo org so the data tells a story: ~15 realistic UK suppliers, bills mostly matchable, 2 deliberate gaps (one missing receipt, one amount mismatch), matching POs raised in Xero, the Google Sheet receipt log schema (columns: date, po_ref, supplier, items, qty, signed_by, photo_link), and idempotency (seed script safe to re-run: check-by-reference before create).

## Step 3 — prove it

Run the `verify-auth.ts` snippet against the real demo org. Then delete nothing, commit `.claude/skills/` and append what you did to `context/last_update.md` per the collaboration protocol.

Hard rules while doing all of this: no business logic in skills (skills are facts + snippets, decisions live in `api/src/modules/`), no UNVERIFIED fact used in Gate-critical paths, and every snippet compiles under our tsconfig.

---

```text
1	# Encode x Xero Hackathon — CONTEXT
2	
3	Read this first. This is the why. `architecture.md` is the how. `prompt.md` bootstraps the Xero skills.
4	
5	## Event facts
6	
7	- Encode Hub x Xero, London. Build deadline Sunday July 5. 3-minute live pitch to judges, in person.
8	- Judges are Xero DevRel/product people (Regan Ashworth owns the Agentic Toolkit, Annie Terry, Sharon Ball). They know JAX, the App Store, and every incumbent. Do not pitch anything JAX or ApprovalMax already does without naming the difference first.
9	- £3,000 per track. Target track: **The Vibe Integrator** (Track 2) — "agent monitors a second app and intelligently syncs the bones of the data into Xero."
10	
11	## Judging rubric (drives every decision)
12	
13	| Weight | Criterion | Our answer |
14	|---|---|---|
15	| 50% | Xero Connection: real problem + strong use of Xero | "Can I pay this?" — the one question every AP process exists to answer. Xero is both the source of truth and the write target. |
16	| 30% | API Integration: effective use of Accounting/Payments APIs | Bills + aged payables via MCP tools, Purchase Orders via raw Accounting API (not in MCP — shows range), evidence archived via Attachments API, approved bills get real Payments created. Reads and writes, both APIs. |
17	| 20% | Architecture: reliable, production-ready design | Deterministic match engine in Postgres. LLM only at the edges (document parsing, validated JSON out, clamped). Full audit trail per decision. OpenAPI contract between apps. |
18	
19	## The product
20	
21	**Payables control agent.** For every bill that lands in Xero (or in the inbox), it assembles the three-way match — purchase order, goods receipt, invoice — from wherever the evidence already lives: Gmail/Outlook, Google Sheets, Drive. Complete match: auto-approve, create the payment in Xero, archive the evidence, log the trail. Broken match: WhatsApp the owner with exactly what's missing and buttons to act (chase supplier / confirm received / hold).
22	
23	One-liner (locked, use verbatim in the pitch):
24	> "Every business asks one question before paying a bill: did we actually order this, and did it actually arrive? We built the agent that answers it from the mess you already have, and only texts you when the answer is no."
25	
26	## Why this wins (the arguments, rehearsed)
27	
28	- **vs ApprovalMax / Zahara / Lightyear / ProcureDesk** (judges WILL ask): they sell a workflow the whole company must adopt — POs raised in their app, deliveries receipted in their app, £118–220/mo. We sell the answer to AP's only question and need read access, not behaviour change. Their architecture is the rigid if-this-then-that thing Track 2 exists to replace.
29	- **vs JAX**: JAX lives inside one Xero org and won't decide what's safe to pay (liability asymmetry — Xero captures no upside from the call and all the blame). We assemble evidence from outside Xero and take a position, with the audit trail to back it.
30	- **vs "ChatGPT could do this"**: opinions are free, accountability is the product. Deterministic matching, evidence chain, approval controls, write-back.
31	
32	## Human story (pitch opening)
33	
34	The site manager never logs into anything. The PO is an email thread. The delivery proof is a photo of a signed note on someone's phone. The invoice is a PDF in a shared inbox. Every incumbent's answer is "make all these people use our app." Ours is "leave everyone alone, we'll read what's already there."
35	
36	## Demo script (3 min, build backwards from this)
37	
38	1. Dashboard: bills in Xero, most already green (matched, auto-approved, payment created) — the product is the silence. ~30s
39	2. Live: supplier invoice email arrives (Make catches it, or direct IMAP fallback). Agent parses PDF, finds the PO in the sheet, finds NO goods receipt. Bill goes amber with the gap named. ~45s
40	3. Judge's/our phone buzzes on WhatsApp: "Bill from Thameside Timber, £4,200 — no delivery confirmation. Chase / Confirm / Hold." Tap chase. Agent emails the site contact. ~45s
41	4. Reply sent live from a phone with a delivery-note photo. Agent reads it, closes the match, bill flips green in OUR ui, then show the approved bill + payment + attached evidence INSIDE Xero. ~45s
42	5. Close on rubric mapping + the ApprovalMax line. ~15s
43	
44	## Scope cuts (already decided, do not reopen)
45	
46	- No multi-org, no accountant view. One demo org.
47	- No quotes anywhere in the demo (Xero's own Lovable example is a quote system; judges are numb to it).
48	- No user management, no settings pages. One hardcoded business.
49	- WhatsApp via Twilio sandbox, built LAST. Numbered-reply fallback if buttons misbehave.
50	- Make.com is plumbing only (OAuth + email trigger + webhook to us). Zero business logic in Make. Direct IMAP poll on the demo mailbox as the stage fallback if Make's trigger latency can't be made instant.
51	- Seed the demo Xero org tonight: ~15 bills, matching POs for most, deliberate gaps for the demo path. Data must tell a story (real supplier names, plausible amounts).
52	
53	## Sponsor visibility (free points)
54	
55	Make (workshop sponsor) used visibly as the connector layer. Lovable/Replit not used — fine. Xero MCP + raw API + Attachments + Payments named explicitly on the architecture slide.
56
```

```text
1	# Payables Control Agent — ARCHITECTURE
2	
3	Read `context.md` first. This file is the build truth.
4	
5	## Stack (locked)
6	
7	- **Frontend:** Next.js (App Router), TypeScript, Tailwind + shadcn/ui. Built by Cursor against the OpenAPI contract. `impeccable` + `ui-ux-pro-max` skills mandatory. `frontend-design` skill prohibited.
8	- **Backend:** NestJS + PostgreSQL + TypeORM. Owns ALL Xero calls, matching, decisions, WhatsApp, audit.
9	- **LLM:** Claude API. Document parsing only (invoice PDFs, delivery-note photos, messy email bodies → strict JSON, zod-validated, clamped). Never in the match/approve decision path.
10	- **Connectors:** Make.com scenario (Gmail watch → attachment + body → POST our webhook). Direct IMAP poll on the demo mailbox as stage fallback, toggled by env flag.
11	- **WhatsApp:** Twilio sandbox. Interactive buttons if templates behave, numbered replies as the guaranteed path.
12	- **Xero:** Custom Connection (client_credentials — no OAuth redirect dance, one org, perfect for demo). MCP server tools where they exist; raw Accounting API for Purchase Orders and Attachments (not in MCP). Payments API for the approve action.
13	
14	## System shape
15	
16	```
17	  Gmail/Outlook ──▶ Make scenario ──▶ POST /webhooks/email ─┐
18	  (IMAP poll fallback, env-flagged) ─────────────────────────┤
19	                                                             ▼
20	                        ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
21	   Google Sheet         │  NestJS                                    │
22	   (goods receipts) ──▶ │  ingest → parse (Claude, zod, clamp)       │
23	                        │  → MATCH ENGINE (pure TS, deterministic)   │
24	   Xero (bills, POs) ─▶ │  → decision + audit row                    │
25	                        │  → green: approve bill, create Payment,    │
26	                        │           attach evidence (Xero APIs)      │
27	                        │  → amber: WhatsApp exception (Twilio)      │
28	                        └───────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
29	                                        │ OpenAPI (generated types)
30	                                        ▼
31	                        Next.js dashboard: bill list, match evidence
32	                        drill-down (the case file), audit trail
33	```
34	
35	## Match engine (the deterministic core — no LLM inside)
36	
37	Entities: `Bill`, `PurchaseOrder`, `GoodsReceipt`, `MatchResult`, `AuditEvent`, `ExceptionAction`.
38	
39	Match logic, in order:
40	1. **PO link:** supplier fuzzy-normalised (LLM normalises names at ingest, engine compares normalised strings) + amount within tolerance (default ±2% or £5, whichever greater) + PO date ≤ invoice date. Line-level qty×price check when lines exist on both.
41	2. **Receipt link:** goods receipt referencing the PO (sheet row or parsed delivery note) with received qty ≥ invoiced qty per line, or total-level if lines absent.
42	3. **Verdict:** `MATCHED` → auto-path. `MISSING_PO` / `MISSING_RECEIPT` / `AMOUNT_MISMATCH` / `QTY_MISMATCH` → exception path with the gap named and the evidence found so far attached.
43	
44	Every verdict writes an `AuditEvent` (input refs, rule hits, tolerances applied, actor=agent|human, timestamp). The audit trail IS the 20% architecture story — surface it in the UI.
45	
46	## Xero API usage map (the 30% story — name these in the pitch)
47	
48	| Call | Via | Purpose |
49	|---|---|---|
50	| list/get invoices (ACCPAY) | MCP tool | ingest bills already in Xero |
51	| create invoice (ACCPAY) | MCP tool | bills arriving by email that aren't in Xero yet |
52	| Purchase Orders CRUD | raw Accounting API | MCP gap — read POs, mark billed |
53	| Attachments | raw Accounting API | archive evidence PDF/photo onto the bill |
54	| approve bill (status update) | MCP tool | flip AUTHORISED on green |
55	| create Payment | MCP/Payments API | the money moment on approval |
56	| aged payables by contact | MCP tool | dashboard context strip |
57	| History/Notes endpoint | raw API | write the agent's decision note onto the bill in Xero |
58	
59	Rate limits: 60 calls/min/tenant, 5,000/day — batch reads, cache org data in Postgres, never poll Xero in a loop.
60	
61	## API contract (frontend ↔ backend)
62	
63	NestJS decorators → Swagger at `/api/docs` → `openapi.json` committed → frontend runs `openapi-typescript` to generate `web/lib/api-types.ts`. The generated file is the ONLY source of request/response types in the frontend. Contract change = regenerate + commit together. No hand-typed fetch shapes, ever.
64	
65	## Repo layout
66	
67	```
68	encode-xero-hack/
69	  context/            # this suite — both agents read before writing
70	  api/                # NestJS (Claude Code owns)
71	    src/modules/{ingest,match,xero,whatsapp,audit}/
72	    openapi.json      # committed contract
73	  web/                # Next.js (Cursor owns)
74	    lib/api-types.ts  # generated, never hand-edited
75	  seed/               # demo-org seeding scripts + sheet fixtures
76	  .claude/skills/     # Xero skills generated via context/prompt.md
77	```
78	
79	## Build order (gates, not suggestions)
80	
81	| Gate | Definition of done |
82	|---|---|
83	| G0 | Custom Connection auth proven: one script lists invoices from the demo org. Skills generated (prompt.md). Demo org seeded (~15 bills, POs in Xero, receipts sheet, 2 deliberate gaps). |
84	| G1 | Match engine passing unit tests against seeded data: greens green, ambers amber, correct gap names. **Highest-risk-first: this is the project.** |
85	| G2 | Xero write path: approve + create Payment + attach evidence + history note, verified inside Xero UI. |
86	| G3 | Email ingest live (Make webhook + IMAP fallback), Claude parse → engine → verdict end to end. |
87	| G4 | Dashboard: bill list + evidence drill-down (the case file view) + audit trail. Contract-generated types only. |
88	| G5 | WhatsApp exception loop (Twilio sandbox): buzz, tap/reply, chase email out, reply-photo closes match. LAST. |
89	| G6 | Three full live rehearsals. Pitch ×2 out loud. Hotspot configured. |
90	
91	Behind at any gate: cut from the bottom. WhatsApp buttons → numbered replies → if desperate, exception actions from the dashboard instead of WhatsApp. The match engine, Xero writes, and evidence UI are untouchable.
92	
93	## Live-demo rules
94	
95	1. Live data only. The demo mailbox, sheet, and Xero org are real; the arriving email and the delivery-note photo are sent live on stage.
96	2. Streaming/optimistic UI so latency reads as the agent thinking.
97	3. Every failure has a rehearsed fallback path that is also live (IMAP instead of Make, numbered replies instead of buttons).
98	4. Demoable at every commit. Never broken at the top of any hour.
99	
100	## Two-agent protocol
101	
102	Claude Code owns `api/`, `seed/`, skills. Cursor owns `web/`. Both read `context/last_update.md` before any write (hook-managed). Conflicts resolve in favour of the latest entry holder. The OpenAPI contract is the only interface — neither agent reaches into the other's tree.
103
```
</details></tool-use>

Scaffolding is in place. The multi-agent workflow is running in the background; when it completes I'll apply the fact-checkers' corrections, compile-check every snippet, attempt Gate G0, and commit.

Status so far:

- **Cloned** all four XeroAPI repos into `.tmp/xero-refs/` and added `.tmp/` to `.gitignore` (it wasn't ignored).
- **Confirmed the core MCP-gap fact directly**: the MCP server's 51 handlers include invoices, contacts, payments, and aged-payables tools, but nothing for Purchase Orders, Attachments, or History/Notes — those must go through the raw Accounting API, exactly as `architecture.md` assumes.
- **Workflow launched** (`xero-skills-build`): 7 parallel fact-miners (MCP server internals, prompt-library style discipline, the AP Symfony prompt, agent-toolkit/CLI auth patterns, plus 3 agents verifying against official developer.xero.com docs), then one author per skill working only from those fact sheets, then one adversarial fact-checker per skill trying to refute every claim against the sources.
- **Credentials gap found**: there is no `.env` and no Xero credentials anywhere on this machine, so the Gate G0 live auth check will be blocked until you create the Custom Connection app. I've written `.env.example` at the repo root with the exact scopes and a pointer to the verify command.

11	      "style": "WROTE undefined/style.md (absolute: /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/style.md)\n\n- Read all 5 target docs plus replit single-account skill (it is a plain file with SKILL.md frontmatter).\n- Structure: frontmatter is name+description only; description = what/when/covers/read-first + routing to sibling skill; fixed section order ending in a numbered Verify list (with steps 0 and 0.5).\n- Pre-flight discipline captured verbatim: requireEnv non-empty assertion, client_id_len logging (never the secret), smoke-test authorize URL before clicking, 32-byte TOKEN_ENC_KEY validation.\n- Error->cause gold copied verbatim: unauthorized_client <- client_id=undefined; invalid redirect_uri <- 3 origin traps + port/trailing-slash; 403 <- read-only scope (unfixable without re-consent); 500-on-callback <- bad enc key after single-use code burned; 401 \"No connection found for replid\"; 404 <- alias paths; 400 <- reused idempotency key with different body; cached errors; 429/Retry-After.\n- Scope minimalism: additive/minimum/re-consent rule, granular-over-broad migration tables, 3 exact scope strings.\n- Tokens: access ~30 min, refresh ~60 days rotating, 60s/120s early-refresh buffers, expires_in*1000 persistence, sibling-row rotation, needs_reconnect state, never-log/never-expose rules.\n- Section 7 isolates Lovable/Supabase/Replit-only guidance (createServerFn, Edge Functions/Deno, RLS/service_role, redirect resolver, connector proxy) that must not enter NestJS, plus a keep-list of portable rules.",
12	      "ap": "WROTE /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/ap-symfony.md\n\n- Both source files are 16-line app-idea prompts, not API docs; nearly zero field-level Xero detail.\n- Only hard API fact (verbatim): \"Key Endpoints: /Invoices (bills), /PurchaseOrders, /Payments, /Contacts\" — confirms bills live on /Invoices (ACCPAY implied, not stated).\n- Laravel file adds /OnlineInvoices and the webhook fact: \"webhook endpoints for Xero invoice updates ... two-way sync\".\n- No bill statuses named anywhere (no DRAFT/AUTHORISED/etc.), no payment fields, no where-filters, no pagination, no rate limits.\n- Payment execution is explicitly external (\"integration with banking APIs for payment execution\") — Xero /Payments is the record, not the rail.\n- No concrete seed data (no suppliers/amounts), but shape hints: PO-correlated bills, near-duplicate invoices for fuzzy matching, varied payment terms with early-pay discounts.\n- Approval workflows, three-way match, duplicate detection are framed as app-side features, which supports our external match-engine design.\n- Verdict: too thin to source status flows or field names; use the API reference/MCP server code for those.",
13	      "toolkit": "WROTE /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/auth-toolkit.md\n\n- xero-agent-toolkit has ZERO auth code and ZERO own tools; it just spawns @xeroapi/xero-mcp-server with XERO_CLIENT_ID/SECRET env vars.\n- Real client_credentials flow (in the delegated MCP server): POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token, HTTP Basic base64(id:secret), form body `grant_type=client_credentials&scope=...`; scopes ARE sent (XERO_SCOPES env, else legacy V1 set, fallback granular V2 on invalid_scope).\n- Custom-connection tenant id: fetched from GET api.xero.com/connections after every grant, first tenant used, passed as xero-tenant-id via xero-node; one `tenantId || \"\"` empty fallback for getOrganisations.\n- MCP server does NO token caching — fresh token + /connections call per tool invocation. Our api/ module should cache with expiry buffer instead.\n- Toolkit/MCP surface (50 tools): NO purchase orders, NO attachments, NO history; payments = create-payment + list-payments only. POs/attachments need raw Accounting API as planned.\n- xero-command-line uses PKCE browser OAuth (no secret, client_id in body), AES-256-GCM token cache, 60s pre-expiry refresh, refresh-on-401 retry loop (maxRetries=2).\n- 429 handling (CLI only): no auto-retry; reads retry-after header, regex fallback, default 60s. MCP server just returns a friendly 429 string.\n- Both use xero-node SDK for API calls; raw axios/fetch only for identity endpoints. CLI also injects a versioned user-agent header — worth copying.",
14	      "docs-auth": "WROTE undefined/docs-auth.md (absolute: /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-auth.md)\n\n- Custom Connections: POST https://identity.xero.com/connect/token, grant_type=client_credentials, scopes set at app creation AND passed space-separated in token body; token = JWT, \"valid for up to 30 minutes\" (token-types page); bound to \"a single Xero organisation\" (quoted).\n- xero-tenant-id: current custom-connections page never states it explicitly; call example omits the header (only Authorization + Accept) — explicit sentence marked UNVERIFIED; standard-token requirement quoted from token-types page.\n- Rate limits: 5 concurrent / 60 per min / 1,000-5,000 per day per tenant; 10,000 per min app-wide; headers X-DayLimit-Remaining, X-MinLimit-Remaining, X-AppMinLimit-Remaining; 429 + X-Rate-Limit-Problem + Retry-After (seconds, fixed window) all quoted.\n- Conventions: page/pageSize pagination (default 100, max 1000, 9 endpoints listed), If-Modified-Since semantics + UpdatedDateUTC caveat, 3 exact where examples incl. Type==\"BANK\" and DateTime(2015, 01, 01), order + DESC, summarizeErrors=false, Elements[].ValidationErrors 400 envelope quoted from Response Codes page.\n- Idempotency: Idempotency-Key header supported on POST/PUT/PATCH, 6-min key cache, 128-char max, per-app scope, errors cached, counts against rate limits (official Idempotent Requests guide).\n- Method note: all pages are JS-rendered; WebFetch/curl got empty shells, so content was extracted from Chrome-rendered DOM of official developer.xero.com pages. 15	      "docs-accounting": "WROTE undefined/docs-accounting.md (absolute: /Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-accounting.md)\n\n- All 4 official pages verified (WebFetch timed out; rendered via Chrome directly from developer.xero.com URLs).\n- Invoices: create needs Type+Contact(ContactID)+LineItems; POST=create/update, PUT=create-only; legal transitions DRAFT<->SUBMITTED->AUTHORISED->VOIDED only, DELETED only from DRAFT/SUBMITTED; PAID is system-set; locked-period bills not updatable.\n- ACCPAY InvoiceNumber is NON-unique (shows as Reference in UI) — matters for idempotency.\n- Filtering: where=Type==\"ACCPAY\" AND Status==\"AUTHORISED\" is an official optimised example; Statuses/IDs/ContactIDs list params; page=100 default, invoice max pageSize UNVERIFIED (PO/Payments pages state up to 1000).\n- LineAmountTypes verbatim: Exclusive / Inclusive / NoTax; default Exclusive.\n- POs: statuses DRAFT/SUBMITTED/AUTHORISED/BILLED/DELETED; status changed via POST with PurchaseOrderID; mark-as-billed transition rules UNVERIFIED (no transition table published); no `where` param listed for GET.\n- Payments: PUT with Invoice.InvoiceID + Account (Code or AccountID) + Date + Amount; account must be BANK type or payments-enabled; Amount <= outstanding (partials OK, PAID auto when settled); invoice must be approved (AUTHORISED); reverse via POST /Payments/{id} {\"Status\":\"DELETED\"}; payments immutable otherwise; BankAmount preferred over CurrencyRate for multicurrency.\n- Types page has no CurrencyRate/currency-list section; that and other gaps are tagged UNVERIFIED in the sheet.",
16	      "docs-evidence": "WROTE undefined/docs-evidence.md (/Users/devansh/hackathonProjects/encode-xero-hack/.tmp/xero-refs/undefined/docs-evidence.md)\n- WebFetch timed out 2x on all URLs; extracted official markdown from Gatsby page-data JSON of each docs page instead (verbatim source content).\n- Attachments: POST/PUT raw bytes to /api.xro/2.0/{Endpoint}/{Guid}/Attachments/{Filename}, Content-Type = file MIME; 10 attachments/document; size cap CONFLICTS in official docs (Attachments page: 10MB, Contacts page: 25MB) — flagged, assume 10MB.\n- Filename chars rejected as Bad Request: < > : \" / \\ | ? * \\0 +; encode brackets only.\n- IncludeOnline=true on upload; AR invoices and AR credit notes only.\n- Supported entities: Invoices, Receipts, CreditNotes, RepeatingInvoices, BankTransactions, BankTransfers, Contacts, Accounts, ManualJournals, PurchaseOrders, Quotes.\n- History: GET/PUT/POST {Endpoint}/{Guid}/history; PUT body {\"HistoryRecords\":[{\"Details\":...}]}, Details max 2500 chars; cannot update notes, always \"System Generated\" + creation date (no backdating); 14 supported doc types listed.\n- Reports: GET /Reports/AgedPayablesByContact, contactID REQUIRED; optional date/fromDate/toDate; Rows RowType Header/Section/Row/SummaryRow, cells carry invoiceID Attributes.\n- Contacts: Name max 255 (uniqueness being deprecated — use ContactID), optimised where= equals on Name/EmailAddress/AccountNumber, searchTerm searches 6 fields, page/pageSize (default 100), IsSupplier read-only auto-set by AP invoices, summaryOnly EXCLUDES IsSupplier.\n- ContactStatus values ACTIVE/ARCHIVED/GDPRREQUEST confirmed from official Types page."

_**Agent (claude-fable-5 2026-07-04 12:19:27Z)**_

All five fix agents are running in parallel. I'll get notified as each completes; once all five are done I'll compile-check every snippet, run a consistency sweep across the skills, attempt Gate G0, and commit.

**Summary of what the adversarial verification caught** (the reason this pass was worth it):

- **Demo-killer**: the seed spec put the PO join key in the bill's `Reference` field — but `Reference` is ACCREC-only and Xero silently drops it on ACCPAY bills. Every seeded bill would have come back with no PO link and all 15 would verdict MISSING_PO at Gate G1. The fix moves the PO ref into line-item text (realistic supplier phrasing) with `InvoiceNumber` as the idempotency key.
- **Gate-blocker**: the seed preflight checked org identity via `GET /Organisation`, which 403s under our four scopes (needs `accounting.settings.read`) — the seed could never start. Switched to `GET /connections`.
- **Judged-moment failure**: the payments skill documented `ts-node`, which errors outright on Node 22 (`ERR_UNKNOWN_FILE_EXTENSION`), and its script never loaded `.env`, so the "must work first try" payment snippet failed on try one, twice over. Also: its idempotency check silently skipped when the optional reference arg was omitted, violating the every-write-idempotent rule.
- **Story-breaker**: the amount-mismatch demo gap was qty-shaped (27 vs 25 at the same unit price), which a faithful match engine names QTY_MISMATCH, not AMOUNT_MISMATCH — the G1 test would disagree with the engine. Now price-shaped.
- **Quota trap**: three skills stated 5,000 calls/day, but new Xero apps default to the starter tier at 1,000/day — off by 5x for exactly the app we'll create tomorrow.
- Plus gold mined from the MCP server source: `create-invoice` remaps `reference` → `InvoiceNumber` for ACCPAY (Reference left empty), hardcodes DueDate to today+30 with no param, and `update-invoice` refuses non-DRAFT invoices — so approving bills must go through the raw API.

- Correction 4: pre-flight step 2 now requires the granted scope to "list exactly the whole scope tokens", compared as a Set, with the `.read`-substring false-pass hazard spelled out.
- Correction 5: rate budget restated with explicit batched lookups — ONE `GET /Invoices?InvoiceNumbers=&lt;all 15&gt;&amp;page=1` + ONE cached `GET /PurchaseOrders?page=1&amp;pageSize=100` matched client-side; real arithmetic = 35 calls, with the ≈64-call per-row anti-pattern called out. Idempotency table Bill/PO rows updated (probe vs batch forms, fetch-once), plus a new batch-the-lookups rule.
- Correction 6: "1,000/day on Starter (new-app default) or 5,000/day on higher tiers — read `X-DayLimit-Remaining`, do not assume 5,000."
- Cross-cutting: SEED_SCOPES now `accounting.transactions accounting.contacts` documented as a strict subset of the app's four assigned scopes (invalid_scope otherwise); runner is `npx tsx` and the Snippets section states the .env parser canon; both truncated UNVERIFIED markers fixed (contacts batch envelope, DELETED-PO number reuse) plus one in the sources trail; Sources comment paths moved to `.tmp/xero-refs/facts/*.md` and new evidence lines added (OpenAPI Reference ACCREC-only, /Organisation scope requirement); token-error table now canon: 400 invalid_request (empty id) / invalid_client (wrong creds) / unauthorized_client (not a Custom Connection) / invalid_scope (outside assigned set).
- Triage: DELETED/VOIDED-as-absent rule now applies to contacts and bills only, with an explicit PO carve-out pointing at the fresh-number-series rule so §Idempotency and §Resetting agree; "1 token" removed from the 60/min arithmetic (identity.xero.com, counted-or-not marked UNVERIFIED).

**scripts/preflight.ts**
- Org check replaced with `GET https://api.xero.com/connections` (prints every connection's tenantName, throws OrgCheckFailed on non-200 or empty array); closing log tells the human to confirm the DEMO org and check GBP in the Xero UI.
- SEED_SCOPES → `accounting.transactions accounting.contacts` with the subset rationale; scope check is now a whole-token Set comparison (`new Set(token.scope.split(/\s+/))`), MissingScope message notes ".read variants do not count".
- Canonical .env parser added (comments/blanks, leading `export `, matching-quote strip, process.env wins), loaded from repo root; header documents `npx tsx` as the runner; MissingEnv message now cites 400 invalid_request.

**scripts/check-by-reference.ts**
- `findPurchaseOrderByNumber` restructured into `fetchPurchaseOrdersPage` (one list GET) + a pure client-side match taking the prefetched list, so a per-row transcription physically cannot re-fetch; main() fetches once and reuses.
- New `findBillsByInvoiceNumbers` batched lookup (`InvoiceNumbers=` comma-separated param, one call for all 15, Map keyed by every requested ref, ACCPAY filtered client-side); the where-filter version is documented as the one-off probe only.
- Same cross-cutting upgrades as preflight: .env parser, `npx tsx` runner, new SEED_SCOPES, invalid_request in MissingEnv, header comment rewritten around probe-vs-batch and fetch-once.

Sanity constraints held: every bill £84.60–£5,940.00 net (inside £80–£8,000), every gross exact at 2dp under LineAmountTypes Exclusive ×1.2 (verified programmatically), and the cast table remains a pure transcription job (the only derivation rule, the bill Description suffix, is stated once directly above it).</result>

_**Agent (claude-fable-5 2026-07-04 12:32:21Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-fable-5 2026-07-04 12:32:21Z)**_

Done. The five Xero skills are built, adversarially fact-checked, compile-verified, and committed. One thing remains that only you can do: create the Xero Custom Connection app so the live Gate G0 check can run.

## What was built

**`.claude/skills/xero-auth`** — client_credentials token flow over plain fetch (no xero-node, no tenant id header), the four project scopes and what each unlocks, an in-memory token cache module shaped for a NestJS provider, and `scripts/verify-auth.ts` (the Gate G0 script: parses `.env` itself, fails fast with named errors, prints org name + invoice count + rate-limit headers).

**`.claude/skills/xero-accounting-api`** — the raw API surface for ACCPAY bills, Purchase Orders, Attachments, History notes, Contacts, and the AgedPayablesByContact report, with a shared `xeroFetch` helper that handles 429/Retry-After and the `Elements[].ValidationErrors` envelope. POs/Attachments/History are loudly marked as MCP gaps.

**`.claude/skills/xero-payments`** — the judged Payments moment: `create-payment.ts` pre-flights that the invoice is AUTHORISED with sufficient AmountDue, always runs a check-by-reference before writing, and uses an Idempotency-Key with a salt escape hatch for Xero's cached-error window.

**`.claude/skills/xero-mcp-usage`** — the MCP-vs-raw decision table, exact tool names and params for the ~10 tools we use, and the hard limitations mined from the server source: `update-invoice` refuses non-DRAFT bills (approval must go raw), `create-invoice` remaps `reference`→`InvoiceNumber` for ACCPAY and hardcodes DueDate to today+30, `type` is required despite the description claiming a default.

**`.claude/skills/xero-demo-seed`** — the full 15-supplier UK cast as a transcription-ready table, exactly two deliberate gaps (Thameside Timber £4,200 no-receipt; one bill £148 over PO, price-shaped so it can't read as QTY_MISMATCH), the sheet schema, and idempotent batched lookups.

## Why the verification pass mattered

The adversarial fact-checkers refuted 23 claims with evidence, the worst being genuine demo-killers: the seed originally carried the PO join key in `Reference`, which Xero **silently drops on ACCPAY bills** (all 15 bills would have verdicted MISSING_PO at G1 — the join now rides in line-item text); the seed preflight used `/Organisation`, which 403s under our scopes; the payments script documented a runner that errors on Node 22 and never loaded `.env`; and three skills quoted 5,000 calls/day when new apps get the 1,000/day starter tier.

## State of the gates

- All 11 snippets compile under strict ES2022/commonjs; consistency sweep across skills is clean.
- `verify-auth.ts` fail-fast path proven (named MissingEnv error, correct guidance).
- **Blocked on you**: create the Custom Connection at developer.xero.com/app/manage with the four scopes listed in `.env.example`, have the org admin authorise it, fill in `.env`, then run `npx tsx .claude/skills/xero-auth/scripts/verify-auth.ts` — that completes G0.

Also worth knowing: `context/architecture.md:59` states 5,000/day as the rate limit; it's tier-dependent and a fresh app gets 1,000/day. Worth a one-line correction there (I left the file alone since it's the shared context suite). For Cursor: the five `xero-*` skills are the Xero source of truth from here on — no rediscovering Xero facts mid-build.

_**Agent (claude-fable-5 2026-07-04 22:31:05Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./HANDOFF.md`

```text
1	# Robyn — HANDOFF
2	
3	Robyn turns a freelancer's calendar, contracts and transcripts into invoices in Xero.
4	Backend (NestJS, `api/`) + frontend (Next.js, `web/`) + demo seed (`seed/`).
5	
6	---
7	
8	## ✅ Xero is LIVE (creds fixed)
9	
10	The Xero Custom Connection is connected to **Demo Company (UK)** and the whole money moment is verified live: approving a proposal wrote **INV-0068** (ACCREC, AUTHORISED, £450, Net 14) with Robyn's decision note in History and two evidence attachments (contract clause + transcript excerpt). The Connections panel shows XERO as LIVE. `pnpm --filter robyn-api verify:xero` prints `G0 PASS`.
11	
12	If you ever need to re-point Xero at a different org, the original setup steps are below.
13	
14	<details><summary>How the Custom Connection was set up (for reference / re-doing)</summary>
15	
16	The first creds were a standard OAuth2 app (client_credentials filtered out every accounting scope, `/connections` 400'd). The fix was a **fresh Custom Connection**:
17	1. Go to <https://developer.xero.com/> → **My Apps** → **New app** → choose **Custom Connection** (you cannot convert the existing standard app — it must be a fresh Custom Connection app).
18	2. Name it (e.g. "Robyn"). For the authorising user, use yourself.
19	3. **Scopes** — select the accounting scopes. Either the broad set (`accounting.transactions`, `accounting.contacts`, `accounting.attachments`, `accounting.settings`, `accounting.reports.read`) or, if the picker only offers granular scopes, select: `accounting.invoices`, `accounting.contacts`, `accounting.attachments`, `accounting.settings.read`, `accounting.payments`, `accounting.reports.read`. (Robyn's token module tries broad first, then falls back to granular automatically.)
20	4. Save. You'll get an email / an in-portal **Connect** button — click it and **connect the app to your Xero *Demo Company*** (Custom Connections are **free** against the Demo Company). Pick exactly one org.
21	5. Copy the app's **Client ID** and **Client Secret**.
22	6. Paste them into `api/.env` (replace the existing `XERO_CLIENT_ID` / `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET`). Do not commit this file — it is gitignored and irreplaceable.
23	7. Verify: `cd api && npx tsx src/modules/xero/verify.ts` — expect `G0 PASS` with your org name and an invoice count.
24	
25	Once `G0 PASS`, every Xero path in Robyn goes live automatically (contact create, ACCREC invoice draft/authorise, History note, Attachment, Payments read). Then run the Xero seed: `pnpm --filter robyn-api seed`.
26	
27	If the picker won't grant a scope the fallback expects, set `XERO_SCOPES=` in `api/.env` to the *exact* space-separated scopes the app was granted, and re-run verify. (This app is granular-only; the token module requests broad, then the granular set, then an empty scope which grants exactly what the app has.)
28	
29	</details>
30	
31	---
32	
33	## Run it
34	
35	```bash
36	# from repo root
37	make db          # or: docker compose up -d db   (Postgres on :5432)
38	pnpm install
39	pnpm --filter robyn-api seed     # populates the demo (local always; Xero when creds are live)
40	pnpm dev         # api on :4000 (docs /api/docs), web on :3000
41	```
42	
43	- API alone: `pnpm dev:api`  ·  Web alone: `pnpm dev:web`
44	- Regenerate the API contract after a backend change: `pnpm openapi` (writes `api/openapi.json` + `web/lib/api-types.ts`).
45	
46	## Credentials you must add to `api/.env`
47	
48	| Var | Needed for | Without it |
49	|---|---|---|
50	| `XERO_CLIENT_ID` / `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` | live Xero (see above) | dashboard still demos from local seed; writes surface a clear error until fixed |
51	| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | transcript/contract/email parsing | present ✓ |
52	| `IMAP_HOST/PORT/USER/PASSWORD` | live email poll | **empty → fixture mailbox fallback** (`seed/data/mailbox/`); Loop 2 still demos |
53	| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET` | live Google Calendar | **empty → `.ics` fallback** (`seed/data/calendar.ics`); Loop 1 still demos |
54	| `XERO_PAYMENT_ACCOUNT_CODE` | recording a Payment write | optional; Payments are read-only in the core demo |
55	
56	## Fallbacks (missing-credential protocol)
57	- **Email**: IMAP creds empty → the poll reads a fixture mailbox behind the same interface, filtered to queued prospect addresses only. Connections panel shows EMAIL as FALLBACK truthfully.
58	- **Calendar**: Google creds empty → `.ics` upload / seeded `.ics`, identical pipeline. Connections shows CALENDAR as FALLBACK.
59	- To go live: paste the creds, restart the API, hit "check now" on the Connections panel.
60	
61	## Demo runbook (3 minutes, verified end to end)
62	
63	Start clean: `pnpm --filter robyn-api seed` then `pnpm dev`, open <http://localhost:3000>. The cold open shows **3 tasks** and the leak strip **"£2,880 recoverable in June 2026"**.
64	
65	1. **Cold open on the Tasks inbox (20s).** Three cards waiting: a meeting missing its transcript, an agreement email to confirm, an invoice in review. "This is my back office now." Point at the leak strip.
66	2. **Rule 1 live — transcript to invoice (70s).** On the *Provide transcript* card (Fenwick kitchen fit-out review), paste a Granola transcript that includes an extra ask (e.g. "can you also spec the utility room, that is new work"). Robyn matches the client, pulls the contract, and builds the proposal: **1.5h calendar block @ £150 cited to Clause 3.1 + the utility room 3h caught from the transcript with the verbatim quote = £810 inc. VAT**. Open *Show evidence*, then **Approve & send to Xero** → the invoice is created live in Xero (AUTHORISED) with Robyn's decision note in History and the transcript + contract-clause attachments. Follow the Xero deep link to show it in the org.
67	3. **Rule 2 live — email to client (50s).** The *Confirm agreement* card shows Priya Nair's verbatim "Let's go ahead." **Confirm and create client** → Robyn creates the Xero contact and onboards Priya, then asks for her contract. New client from a calendar event and an email, zero forms.
68	4. **Auto-send + close (40s).** Open **Clients** → Halcyon Retail has autonomy **ON**: its monthly retainer invoice goes out on its own within contract terms (the 5 auto-sent history invoices are on the card, live in the org). Leak strip: "June: £2,880 was walking away." Map to the rubric: real problem + Xero depth (50%), Accounting/Payments API (30%), production-ready architecture — the Connections panel and audit trail (20%).
69	
70	Supporting surfaces to show if asked: **Calendar** (every block colour-coded by whether it's been paid for; click any event for its evidence chain), **Invoices** (every line's provenance chip + the "Money Robyn found" ledger detections), **Connections** (honest LIVE/FALLBACK/DOWN health).
71	
72	## What is verified live vs via fallback
73	
74	- **Live now, verified end to end against Xero (Demo Company UK):** contact create, ACCREC invoice create + authorise, History decision note, evidence Attachments, Payments (Halcyon retainer cadence with the June gap), an accepted Quote, and aged receivables. Proof: approving a proposal wrote **INV-0068** with its note + 2 attachments. The seed mirrors the whole story into the org (3 contacts, 17 invoices, quote QU-0042, 5 payments).
75	- **Live now:** the reconciliation engine (29 unit tests), Loop 1 transcript→invoice with the real Anthropic LLM, Loop 2 email agreement detection, Loop 3 detectors, the whole dashboard, all state transitions and the audit trail. Screenshots of every beat are in `docs/qa/`.
76	- **Fallback by design (no creds present):** calendar via the seeded `.ics`, email via the fixture mailbox. Both are shown honestly as FALLBACK on the Connections panel and swap to live when you add Google/IMAP creds.
77	
78	## Three things to check first when you land
79	
80	1. **Confirm Xero is still live** — `pnpm --filter robyn-api verify:xero` should print `G0 PASS`; the Connections panel should show XERO **LIVE / Demo Company (UK)**. (Custom Connection tokens don't expire the connection; if it ever drops, the fix steps are in the collapsed section above.)
81	2. **Walk the demo runbook once** against your own machine to get the timing, especially pasting your own Granola transcript in beat 2, and click through to the real invoice in Xero at the end.
82	3. **Skim the Connections panel** — it is the judges' production-readiness read; it tells the truth (Xero LIVE, calendar + email on demo data until you add those creds).
83	
84	## Known rough edges
85	
86	- Xero writes are gated on the Custom Connection fix — this is the one human step. Everything else runs unattended.
87	- Google Calendar OAuth is coded (read-only, `googleapis` dynamic import gated on a token) but only the `.ics` path is exercised in the demo. IMAP is coded but only the fixture mailbox is exercised.
88	- The LLM transcript parse is live and conservative (only work *beyond* the meeting time is billed), but output quality depends on the transcript — the human always reviews unless autonomy is ON.
89	- In dev mode React Strict Mode double-fires fetches; you'll see duplicate 200s in DevTools. Harmless; production build does not.
90	- Schema is TypeORM `synchronize` (no migrations) — fine for the demo, not for production.
91
```

---compose---
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    container_name: robyn-db
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: robyn
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: robyn
      POSTGRES_DB: robyn
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - robyn-pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U robyn -d robyn"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10

volumes:
  robyn-pgdata:
---Makefile---
.PHONY: dev db seed openapi install build stop verify-xero

install:
	pnpm install

db:
	docker compose up -d db

stop:
	docker compose down

# One command to bring the whole thing up (db + api + web)
dev: db
	pnpm dev

seed:
	pnpm seed

openapi:
	pnpm openapi

build:
	pnpm build

# Live Xero Custom Connection check (Gate G0)
verify-xero:
	cd api && npx tsx src/modules/xero/verify.ts
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./api/src/config/env.ts`

```text
1	// Central env access. Required secrets are asserted non-empty at boot with
2	// named errors so a misconfigured process dies at startup, not mid-demo.
3	// Values are read from api/.env (loaded by @nestjs/config in AppModule).
4	
5	export class MissingEnvError extends Error {
6	  constructor(name: string) {
7	    super(
8	      `MissingEnv: ${name} is empty — set it in api/.env. Robyn refuses to boot ` +
9	        `without it rather than fail obscurely later.`,
10	    );
11	    this.name = 'MissingEnvError';
12	  }
13	}
14	
15	function req(name: string): string {
16	  const v = process.env[name];
17	  if (!v || v.trim() === '') throw new MissingEnvError(name);
18	  return v.trim();
19	}
20	
21	function opt(name: string, fallback = ''): string {
22	  const v = process.env[name];
23	  return v && v.trim() !== '' ? v.trim() : fallback;
24	}
25	
26	export interface RobynConfig {
27	  port: number;
28	  databaseUrl: string;
29	  anthropicApiKey: string;
30	  xero: {
31	    clientId: string;
32	    clientSecret: string;
33	    scopes: string; // XERO_SCOPES override (empty => broad-then-granular fallback)
34	    paymentAccountCode: string; // optional; needed only for Payments writes
35	  };
36	  imap: {
37	    host: string;
38	    port: number;
39	    user: string;
40	    password: string;
41	    configured: boolean;
42	  };
43	  google: {
44	    clientId: string;
45	    clientSecret: string;
46	    configured: boolean;
47	  };
48	}
49	
50	let cached: RobynConfig | null = null;
51	
52	// Assert the hard-required secrets. Xero + Anthropic + DB are mandatory; IMAP
53	// and Google are optional (documented .ics / fixture-mailbox fallbacks exist).
54	export function loadConfig(): RobynConfig {
55	  if (cached) return cached;
56	  const imapHost = opt('IMAP_HOST');
57	  const imapUser = opt('IMAP_USER');
58	  const imapPassword = opt('IMAP_PASSWORD');
59	  const googleClientId = opt('GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID');
60	  const googleClientSecret = opt('GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET');
61	
62	  cached = {
63	    port: Number(opt('PORT', '4000')),
64	    databaseUrl: req('DATABASE_URL'),
65	    anthropicApiKey: req('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'),
66	    xero: {
67	      clientId: req('XERO_CLIENT_ID'),
68	      clientSecret: req('XERO_CLIENT_SECRET'),
69	      scopes: opt('XERO_SCOPES'),
70	      paymentAccountCode: opt('XERO_PAYMENT_ACCOUNT_CODE'),
71	    },
72	    imap: {
73	      host: imapHost,
74	      port: Number(opt('IMAP_PORT', '993')),
75	      user: imapUser,
76	      password: imapPassword,
77	      configured: Boolean(imapHost && imapUser && imapPassword),
78	    },
79	    google: {
80	      clientId: googleClientId,
81	      clientSecret: googleClientSecret,
82	      configured: Boolean(googleClientId && googleClientSecret),
83	    },
84	  };
85	  return cached;
86	}
87
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-fable-5 2026-07-04 22:35:18Z)**_

Everything that can run without your secrets is now running. Here's where things stand:

## Pulled and running

- **Pulled the full build** — local `main` was 7 commits behind; it fast-forwarded cleanly to `bfe8a38` ("Xero LIVE", on top of the QA-green handoff, tagged `v0-robyn-e2e`). The repo is now the complete Robyn monorepo: NestJS `api/`, Next.js `web/`, `seed/`, QA screenshots in `docs/qa/`, and a HANDOFF.md runbook.
- **Postgres is up and healthy** — on host port **5433**, not 5432, because your `myman-postgres-dev` container owns 5432 and I didn't touch it. The remap lives in a gitignored `docker-compose.override.yml` plus the matching `DATABASE_URL` tweak in `api/.env`, so other machines are unaffected.
- **Dependencies installed**, and the **web app is serving on http://localhost:3000** (HTTP 200).

## Blocked on three secrets only you have

The API refuses to boot — by design, with a named error — because `api/.env` on this machine is just the placeholder template. The remote agent's real credentials never left its machine (`.env` is gitignored). It hard-requires all three of:

| Var | Where to get it |
|---|---|
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | console.anthropic.com → API keys |
| `XERO_CLIENT_ID` / `XERO_CLIENT_SECRET` | developer.xero.com → My Apps → the **Custom Connection** created during the remote session (connected to Demo Company UK). The ID is visible there; if you didn't save the secret, generate a new one on the app's page |

Fastest path: if the remote agent's workspace is still open, its `api/.env` has all three working values — copy them straight across. I checked your shell env, keychain, and Claude settings; no usable keys exist locally.

Paste them into `api/.env` (edit the file directly rather than sending secrets through chat), then tell me and I'll do the rest: restart the servers, run the seed (it needs one API boot first to create the tables — that's why the earlier seed attempt failed, not a bug), and run `verify:xero` to confirm `G0 PASS`.

One discrepancy worth knowing: HANDOFF claims the dashboard demos without Xero creds, but `api/src/config/env.ts:67-68` makes them mandatory at boot. If you want, once you've added the Anthropic key I can loosen the Xero assertion to match the documented fallback behaviour — but with real creds pasted it's moot.

---
